prompt: We're getting 500 support tickets/month about auth issues. Should we rip out our custom JWT auth and migrate to Auth0, or invest 2 sprints hardening what we have? Team is 8 engineers, 2 have auth domain knowledge.

assertions.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 2 challenges are categorized (reliability, complexity, migration-risk, team-capability, cost, dependency, security)
Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2 challenges
Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts: proceed, proceed-with-conditions, prototype-first, defer, reject
Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in (challengesRaised, stressTestsRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesConceded, mitigationsHeld, mitigationsBroken, proposalModified, confidenceScore)
Concrete action items with who/what/when structure
Round 5 either modifies the original proposal or explicitly justifies keeping it unchanged
Round 5 includes fallback/escape-hatch plans for surviving challenges
